description: Evaluates whether the skill correctly answers WCAG/a11y questions, generates proper ARIA code, explains standards accurately, and provides actionable guidance.

evals.prompt:
My modal dialog closes when I press Escape but screen readers don't announce it as a dialog. What's wrong? Here's the code: ```html

Confirm Delete

Are you sure you want to delete this item?

```
evals.expected_output: Should identify: missing role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby pointing to h2, focus management (focus should move into dialog on open and return on close), and explain the full corrected code with all ARIA attributes.

assertions.name: mentions_focus_management
assertions.description: Response addresses focus management (moving focus into/out of dialog)

assertions.name: mentions_aria_labelledby
assertions.description: Response mentions aria-labelledby to associate heading

evals.prompt: What's the minimum color contrast ratio required for normal body text under WCAG 2.1 AA? My text is #6b7280 on #ffffff background. Does it pass?
evals.expected_output: Should state 4.5:1 for normal text at AA. Should calculate or estimate the contrast of #6b7280 on #ffffff (approximately 4.61:1 or similar — just above the AA threshold). Should confirm it passes AA but may note it's close to the boundary.

assertions.name: states_correct_ratio
assertions.description: Response states 4.5:1 as the minimum for normal text AA

evals.prompt: I'm building a custom dropdown menu in React. Users should be able to open it with Enter or Space, navigate options with arrow keys, and close with Escape. What ARIA attributes and keyboard behavior do I need?
evals.expected_output: Should describe the listbox or menu pattern. Should mention: role='combobox' or role='button' + role='listbox'/role='menu', aria-expanded, aria-haspopup, aria-activedescendant or roving tabindex, keyboard: Enter/Space to open, Arrow keys to navigate, Escape to close, Home/End for first/last, and type-ahead optionally.

assertions.name: mentions_correct_role
assertions.description: Mentions appropriate role (listbox, combobox, or menu)

evals.prompt: My company sells software to EU companies. Do we need to be WCAG compliant? What standard applies and by when?
evals.expected_output: Should mention: European Accessibility Act (EAA), EN 301 549, WCAG 2.1 AA, deadline of 28 June 2025 for private sector. Should clarify it applies to B2C digital products and services.

assertions.name: mentions_eaa
assertions.description: Mentions European Accessibility Act or EAA

assertions.name: mentions_wcag_21_aa
assertions.description: Mentions WCAG 2.1 AA as the referenced standard

evals.prompt: I want to add axe-core to my Playwright tests in a Next.js project. Can you give me a working setup with example test code?
evals.expected_output: Should provide: npm install @axe-core/playwright, import checkA11y or AxeBuilder, a working test that navigates to a URL and runs axe, shows how to handle violations, ideally shows how to integrate into a CI check.

assertions.name: provides_code_example
assertions.description: Provides actual code (contains test or it block)

assertions.name: mentions_violations
assertions.description: Shows how to check or report violations

evals.prompt: How do I make my React Native app work with TalkBack on Android? I have a card component that shows an image, title, and price.
evals.expected_output: Should mention: accessible={true} to combine child elements, accessibilityLabel with combined text, accessibilityRole='button' if tappable, minimum touch target 48dp, accessibilityHint for non-obvious actions. Should provide code example.

evals.prompt: What does WCAG 2.4.11 require? We just updated to WCAG 2.2 and I'm not sure what new criteria to check.
evals.expected_output: Should explain SC 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured (Minimum) — that the focused element must not be entirely hidden by sticky/fixed content. Should also mention other new WCAG 2.2 criteria: 2.4.12, 2.4.13, 2.5.7, 2.5.8, 3.2.6, 3.3.7, 3.3.8, 3.3.9. Should note that 4.1.1 was made obsolete.

assertions.name: mentions_other_new_criteria
assertions.description: Mentions at least one other new WCAG 2.2 criterion

evals.prompt: My form validation shows errors in red text below each field, but a JAWS user told me they don't know the field has an error. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend: aria-invalid='true' on the input, aria-describedby pointing to the error message element, role='alert' or aria-live='assertive' for dynamic errors shown after submission, don't rely on color alone (SC 1.4.1). Should provide code example.

assertions.name: mentions_aria_describedby
assertions.description: Mentions aria-describedby to link error to input

assertions.name: mentions_live_region_or_role_alert
assertions.description: Mentions live region or role=alert for dynamic errors

assertions.name: mentions_color_alone
assertions.description: Notes that color alone is insufficient (SC 1.4.1)

evals.prompt: How do I make an SVG icon accessible? It's just a decorative checkmark.
evals.expected_output: Should mention adding `aria-hidden="true"` or `focusable="false"` for decorative SVGs.

evals.prompt:
I'm using a `div` as a button: `
Submit
`. Is this accessible?
evals.expected_output: Should point out missing role, missing keyboard focus (tabindex), and missing keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space), and recommend using a native `<button>` instead.

evals.prompt: What is the difference between WCAG A, AA, and AAA?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that A is minimum, AA is the global standard/target, and AAA is strict and not applicable to all content.

assertions.name: explains_aa_is_standard
assertions.description: Explains AA is the typical target

evals.prompt: How should I structure headings on a webpage?
evals.expected_output: Should mention using exactly one H1, not skipping heading levels, and using them for structural hierarchy, not just visual styling.

evals.prompt: What is an accessible name and how is it calculated?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the accessible name computation (aria-labelledby > aria-label > visible text/content).

evals.prompt: Is there a way to test accessibility automatically in CI/CD?
evals.expected_output: Should list tools like axe-core, pa11y, Lighthouse, or jest-axe.

evals.prompt: How do I make a skip link?
evals.expected_output: Should provide code for a skip to main content link, hidden by default but visible on focus.

assertions.name: mentions_focus
assertions.description: Mentions making it visible on focus

evals.prompt: What are some cognitive accessibility best practices?
evals.expected_output: Should mention plain language, clear error messages, avoiding time limits, or consistent navigation.

evals.prompt: Do I need alt text on every image?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that decorative images need empty alt text (alt=""), while informative images need descriptive alt text.

evals.prompt: What's the difference between aria-hidden and display: none?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that display: none hides from both visual users and screen readers, while aria-hidden hides ONLY from screen readers.

assertions.name: explains_visual_vs_sr
assertions.description: Explains visual vs screen reader hiding

evals.prompt: How do I write a VPAT?
evals.expected_output: Should explain what a VPAT is (Voluntary Product Accessibility Template) and that it creates an ACR (Accessibility Conformance Report), detailing how to fill it out.

evals.prompt: Are carousels accessible?
evals.expected_output: Should warn that carousels are often problematic, and must include a pause/stop button (WCAG 2.2.2), keyboard navigation, and ARIA live regions.
